issue.title: vite-node dev server fails with `connect EINVAL` on macOS — socket path exceeds 104-byte sun_path limit (regression in 4.4.7)

labels.name: repo-724e8420
labels.description: Important issue, should be addressed soon

issue.body:
### Environment

- Nuxt: 4.4.7 (`@nuxt/vite-builder@4.4.7`)
- Works on: 4.4.6
- OS: macOS 26.5
- Node: 26.0.0
- Package manager: pnpm 10.25.0
- Default `TMPDIR` (`/var/folders/<…>/T`)

### Reproduction

Running `nuxt dev` on macOS with the default temp directory and opening the app fails on the first request:

```
ERROR  [request error] [unhandled] [GET] http://localhost:3000/
Error: connect EINVAL /var/folders/0m/<…>/T/nuxt-vite-node-PoNBaH/nuxt-vite-node-10112-<ts>.sock - Local (undefined:undefined)
    at internalConnect (node:net:1169:16)
    at attemptConnection (@nuxt/vite-builder/dist/vite-node.mjs:37:23)
      const socket = net.createConnection(viteNodeOptions.socketPath);
```

### Cause

In 4.4.7, `pickSocketPath` (`@nuxt/vite-builder/dist/index.mjs`) now wraps the socket file in a dedicated `mkdtemp` subdirectory (chmod `0700`) for isolation:

```js
// 4.4.7
const parentDir = fs.mkdtempSync(join(os.tmpdir(), "nuxt-vite-node-"));
fs.chmodSync(parentDir, 448);
return { socketPath: join(parentDir, `${socketName}.sock`), parentDir };
```

In 4.4.6 the socket file was placed directly in `os.tmpdir()`:

```js
// 4.4.6
return join(os.tmpdir(), `${socketName}.sock`);
```

The new intermediate `nuxt-vite-node-XXXXXX/` segment adds ~22 characters. On macOS the default `os.tmpdir()` is already long (`/var/folders/<…>/T`), so the full socket path now overruns the `sockaddr_un.sun_path` limit of **104 bytes**, and `net.createConnection()` fails with `EINVAL`.

Measured with the default macOS temp dir:

- 4.4.6 path length: **88** ✅ (under 104)
- 4.4.7 path length: **110** ❌ (over 104)

This is platform-dependent: shells/CI with a short `TMPDIR` stay under the limit and don't hit it, which is why it doesn't reproduce everywhere.

### Suggested fix

- Use a shorter `mkdtemp` prefix (e.g. `nvn-` instead of `nuxt-vite-node-`), and/or
- Detect when `os.tmpdir()` would make the resulting socket path exceed the platform limit and fall back (skip the subdir, or use a shorter base dir).

### Workaround

Run the dev server with a short `TMPDIR`:

```json
{ "scripts": { "dev": "TMPDIR=/tmp nuxt dev" } }
```

issue.title: fix(nuxt): set route.meta.layout from appLayout route rule (#34305)
issue.body:
When using the `appLayout` experimental feature (configured via route rules), `route.meta.layout` was undefined even though the layout was correctly resolved at render time by `<NuxtLayout>` fallback to `routeRulesMatcher`. This meant any code reading `route.meta.layout` directly would get `undefined`.

This fix sets `to.meta.layout` from `routeRules.appLayout` (if not already set by the page `definePageMeta`) in the `beforeEach` navigation guard, in both `pages/runtime/plugins/router.ts` and `app/plugins/router.ts`.

Closes #34305

comment.body:
forgive the question - but your [pattern of behaviour](https://agentscan.tools/user/derinbarutcu17) doesn't seem organic/human.

if you are, did you check the closed PRs which took the same approach? did you see my comment about why it might not be correct?

issue.title: vite-node dev server fails with `connect EINVAL` on macOS — socket path exceeds 104-byte sun_path limit (regression in 4.4.7)

labels.name: repo-724e8420
labels.description: Important issue, should be addressed soon

issue.body:
### Environment

- Nuxt: 4.4.7 (`@nuxt/vite-builder@4.4.7`)
- Works on: 4.4.6
- OS: macOS 26.5
- Node: 26.0.0
- Package manager: pnpm 10.25.0
- Default `TMPDIR` (`/var/folders/<…>/T`)

### Reproduction

Running `nuxt dev` on macOS with the default temp directory and opening the app fails on the first request:

```
ERROR  [request error] [unhandled] [GET] http://localhost:3000/
Error: connect EINVAL /var/folders/0m/<…>/T/nuxt-vite-node-PoNBaH/nuxt-vite-node-10112-<ts>.sock - Local (undefined:undefined)
    at internalConnect (node:net:1169:16)
    at attemptConnection (@nuxt/vite-builder/dist/vite-node.mjs:37:23)
      const socket = net.createConnection(viteNodeOptions.socketPath);
```

### Cause

In 4.4.7, `pickSocketPath` (`@nuxt/vite-builder/dist/index.mjs`) now wraps the socket file in a dedicated `mkdtemp` subdirectory (chmod `0700`) for isolation:

```js
// 4.4.7
const parentDir = fs.mkdtempSync(join(os.tmpdir(), "nuxt-vite-node-"));
fs.chmodSync(parentDir, 448);
return { socketPath: join(parentDir, `${socketName}.sock`), parentDir };
```

In 4.4.6 the socket file was placed directly in `os.tmpdir()`:

```js
// 4.4.6
return join(os.tmpdir(), `${socketName}.sock`);
```

The new intermediate `nuxt-vite-node-XXXXXX/` segment adds ~22 characters. On macOS the default `os.tmpdir()` is already long (`/var/folders/<…>/T`), so the full socket path now overruns the `sockaddr_un.sun_path` limit of **104 bytes**, and `net.createConnection()` fails with `EINVAL`.

Measured with the default macOS temp dir:

- 4.4.6 path length: **88** ✅ (under 104)
- 4.4.7 path length: **110** ❌ (over 104)

This is platform-dependent: shells/CI with a short `TMPDIR` stay under the limit and don't hit it, which is why it doesn't reproduce everywhere.

### Suggested fix

- Use a shorter `mkdtemp` prefix (e.g. `nvn-` instead of `nuxt-vite-node-`), and/or
- Detect when `os.tmpdir()` would make the resulting socket path exceed the platform limit and fall back (skip the subdir, or use a shorter base dir).

### Workaround

Run the dev server with a short `TMPDIR`:

```json
{ "scripts": { "dev": "TMPDIR=/tmp nuxt dev" } }
```

issue.title: explanation of `200.html` and `404.html`

type.name: Documentation
type.description: Something that needs to be written or updated in the documentation.

issue.body: ### Environment - Operating System: `Darwin` - Node Version: `v16.20.1` - Nuxt Version: `3.6.5` - Nitro Version: `2.5.2` - Package Manager: `pnpm@8.7.4` - Builder: `vite` - User Config: `nitro`, `app`, `ui`, `devtools`, `typescript`, `vue`, `modules`, `content`, `colorMode`, `vite` - Runtime Modules: `@nuxt/content@2.7.2`, `@nuxthq/ui@2.7.0-28233674.a2970cb` - Build Modules: `-` ### Reproduction https://stackblitz.com/edit/github-h3ry36?file=nuxt.config.ts ### Describe the bug Got this config: ```js export default defineNuxtConfig({ nitro: { routeRules: { '/**': { prerender: true }, '/spa': { ssr: false }, }, prerender: { crawlLinks: true, routes: ['/', ], }, }, }) ``` After `pnpm build`, there are no fallback html generated for `spa` page. If this isn't a bug, how to generate the fallback htmls like `nuxt generate`? ### Additional context _No response_ ### Logs _No response_

issue.title: [Feature]: Add hook or option to prevent suspenseKey increment during navigation (stable page content on fast navigation)
issue.body:
## Problem

When navigating quickly between routes (cross-component), `<NuxtPage>` increments `suspenseKey` on every navigation. This recreates `<Suspense>` and tears down the previous component content — even if we want to keep showing it while the new page loads.

**Steps to reproduce:**
1. Navigate A → B (request pending, data not yet loaded)
2. Before B resolves, navigate B → C
3. `suspenseKey++` recreates `<Suspense>`, tears down B's vnode
4. C mounts with `data: null` — blank content flash until C's request resolves

**Current behavior:** blank/broken layout flash for the duration of the final fetch

**Expected behavior:** last successfully rendered page stays visible until the new page fully resolves, then swaps — no blank flash

---

## Context

We want a **url-first navigation model**:
- URL changes immediately on every click ✅
- Old page content stays visible while new page loads ✅
- When final pending page resolves — it replaces the stable content ✅
- No blank flash, no partial renders with `data: null` ✅

---

## What already exists

The mechanism for holding the old vnode already exists in `page.ts`:

```ts
// works for same-component navigation (e.g. brand → brand)
if (!nuxtApp.isHydrating && previousPageKey === key && !willRenderAnotherChild) {
  return vnode
}
```

This works perfectly when `previousPageKey === key`. The request is to extend this to **cross-component** navigation during a pending transition.

---

## Proposed solutions

**Option 1 — Nuxt hook:**
```ts
nuxtApp.hook('page:suspend', ({ previousVnode, resume }) => {
  // hold previous vnode, call resume() when ready to swap
})
```

**Option 2 — Prop on `<NuxtPage>`:**
```vue
<NuxtPage :stable-content="true" />
```

**Option 3 — Expose `suspenseKey` via `provide`** so userland can freeze it externally

---

## Workarounds tried and why they fail

| Approach | Why it fails |
|---|---|
| `onLeave` transition hook holding `done()` | `<Transition>` holds the wrapper but `suspenseKey++` empties the content inside |
| `pageKey` prop freeze | works only when `previousPageKey === key`, not cross-component |
| `getCachedData` / `default()` | fixes data state but doesn't prevent component teardown |
| Custom `RouterView` | breaks `useRoute()` internal state managed by `<NuxtPage>` |

---

## Additional context

- The single-navigation case works correctly — `<Suspense>` holds the previous `#default` content while new content resolves
- The problem is specific to **rapid/concurrent navigation**: second click causes `suspenseKey++` which destroys the pending first component before it resolved, and the second component starts with `data: null`
- `<Suspense>` itself only holds one pending state — this is a Vue limitation, the fix needs to be at the `<NuxtPage>` level

issue.title: Nuxt 4.4.7 node-server output packages incomplete unhead@3.1.1

labels.name: repo-724e8420
labels.description: Important issue, should be addressed soon

issue.body:
### Environment

- Nuxt version: `4.4.7`
- Nitro preset: `node-server`
- Package manager: `pnpm@10.33.1`
- Runtime: Node `v22.22.3`
- Deployment: built on server, run with `node .output/server/index.mjs` via PM2

### Describe the bug

After upgrading to Nuxt `4.4.7`, the production server build completes successfully, but the deployed app returns a runtime 500 on SSR requests.

Runtime error:

```text
Error [ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND]: Cannot find module '/home/stir-project/htdocs/releases/20260602185622/.output/server/node_modules/unhead/dist/server.mjs' imported from /home/stir-project/htdocs/releases/20260602185622/.output/server/chunks/routes/renderer.mjs
```

The generated renderer imports `unhead/server`:

```js
import { createHead, propsToString, renderSSRHead } from 'unhead/server'
```

But the packaged server output contains an incomplete `unhead@3.1.1` package:

```text
.output/server/node_modules/unhead -> .nitro/unhead@3.1.1
```

And inside the packaged `dist` directory only these files are present:

```text
.output/server/node_modules/unhead/dist/scripts.mjs
.output/server/node_modules/unhead/dist/shared/
```

Missing files include:

```text
.output/server/node_modules/unhead/dist/server.mjs
.output/server/node_modules/unhead/dist/index.mjs
.output/server/node_modules/unhead/dist/plugins.mjs
.output/server/node_modules/unhead/dist/utils.mjs
```

However, `.output/server/node_modules/unhead/package.json` still exposes:

```json
{
  "./server": {
    "types": "./dist/server.d.ts",
    "default": "./dist/server.mjs"
  }
}
```

So Node resolves `unhead/server` to a file that does not exist in the generated server output.

### Reproduction details

We have not yet reduced this to a minimal public reproduction, but this is consistently reproducible in our production deploy after the Nuxt `4.4.7` update.

The build itself reports success:

```text
✔ Nuxt Nitro server built
✨ Build complete!
```

But runtime SSR requests fail with the missing module error above.

### Workaround

Pinning/overriding Nuxt back to `4.4.6` fixes the generated `.output` for us.

With Nuxt `4.4.6`:

```text
output unhead 2.1.15
output server.mjs OK
```

The generated output includes:

```text
.output/server/node_modules/unhead/dist/server.mjs
.output/server/node_modules/unhead/dist/index.mjs
.output/server/node_modules/unhead/dist/plugins.mjs
.output/server/node_modules/unhead/dist/utils.mjs
```

So our current workaround is to pin Nuxt to `4.4.6` until this is resolved.

### Expected behavior

The `node-server` production output should include all files referenced by package exports used by the generated renderer, especially `unhead/server -> dist/server.mjs`.

### Actual behavior

Nuxt `4.4.7` generates a server output that references `unhead/server`, packages `unhead@3.1.1`, but only includes a partial `dist` directory. This causes runtime SSR requests to fail with `ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND`.

labels.name: repo-724e8420
labels.description: Important issue, should be addressed soon

issue.title: Nuxt 4.4.7 node-server output packages incomplete unhead@3.1.1

labels.name: repo-724e8420
labels.description: Important issue, should be addressed soon

issue.body:
### Environment

- Nuxt version: `4.4.7`
- Nitro preset: `node-server`
- Package manager: `pnpm@10.33.1`
- Runtime: Node `v22.22.3`
- Deployment: built on server, run with `node .output/server/index.mjs` via PM2

### Describe the bug

After upgrading to Nuxt `4.4.7`, the production server build completes successfully, but the deployed app returns a runtime 500 on SSR requests.

Runtime error:

```text
Error [ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND]: Cannot find module '/home/stir-project/htdocs/releases/20260602185622/.output/server/node_modules/unhead/dist/server.mjs' imported from /home/stir-project/htdocs/releases/20260602185622/.output/server/chunks/routes/renderer.mjs
```

The generated renderer imports `unhead/server`:

```js
import { createHead, propsToString, renderSSRHead } from 'unhead/server'
```

But the packaged server output contains an incomplete `unhead@3.1.1` package:

```text
.output/server/node_modules/unhead -> .nitro/unhead@3.1.1
```

And inside the packaged `dist` directory only these files are present:

```text
.output/server/node_modules/unhead/dist/scripts.mjs
.output/server/node_modules/unhead/dist/shared/
```

Missing files include:

```text
.output/server/node_modules/unhead/dist/server.mjs
.output/server/node_modules/unhead/dist/index.mjs
.output/server/node_modules/unhead/dist/plugins.mjs
.output/server/node_modules/unhead/dist/utils.mjs
```

However, `.output/server/node_modules/unhead/package.json` still exposes:

```json
{
  "./server": {
    "types": "./dist/server.d.ts",
    "default": "./dist/server.mjs"
  }
}
```

So Node resolves `unhead/server` to a file that does not exist in the generated server output.

### Reproduction details

We have not yet reduced this to a minimal public reproduction, but this is consistently reproducible in our production deploy after the Nuxt `4.4.7` update.

The build itself reports success:

```text
✔ Nuxt Nitro server built
✨ Build complete!
```

But runtime SSR requests fail with the missing module error above.

### Workaround

Pinning/overriding Nuxt back to `4.4.6` fixes the generated `.output` for us.

With Nuxt `4.4.6`:

```text
output unhead 2.1.15
output server.mjs OK
```

The generated output includes:

```text
.output/server/node_modules/unhead/dist/server.mjs
.output/server/node_modules/unhead/dist/index.mjs
.output/server/node_modules/unhead/dist/plugins.mjs
.output/server/node_modules/unhead/dist/utils.mjs
```

So our current workaround is to pin Nuxt to `4.4.6` until this is resolved.

### Expected behavior

The `node-server` production output should include all files referenced by package exports used by the generated renderer, especially `unhead/server -> dist/server.mjs`.

### Actual behavior

Nuxt `4.4.7` generates a server output that references `unhead/server`, packages `unhead@3.1.1`, but only includes a partial `dist` directory. This causes runtime SSR requests to fail with `ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND`.

Missing files include:

```text
.output/server/node_modules/unhead/dist/server.mjs
.output/server/node_modules/unhead/dist/index.mjs
.output/server/node_modules/unhead/dist/plugins.mjs
.output/server/node_modules/unhead/dist/utils.mjs
```

However, `.output/server/node_modules/unhead/package.json` still exposes:

```json
{
  "./server": {
    "types": "./dist/server.d.ts",
    "default": "./dist/server.mjs"
  }
}
```

So Node resolves `unhead/server` to a file that does not exist in the generated server output.

### Reproduction details

We have not yet reduced this to a minimal public reproduction, but this is consistently reproducible in our production deploy after the Nuxt `4.4.7` update.

The build itself reports success:

```text
✔ Nuxt Nitro server built
✨ Build complete!
```

But runtime SSR requests fail with the missing module error above.

### Workaround

Pinning/overriding Nuxt back to `4.4.6` fixes the generated `.output` for us.

With Nuxt `4.4.6`:

```text
output unhead 2.1.15
output server.mjs OK
```

The generated output includes:

```text
.output/server/node_modules/unhead/dist/server.mjs
.output/server/node_modules/unhead/dist/index.mjs
.output/server/node_modules/unhead/dist/plugins.mjs
.output/server/node_modules/unhead/dist/utils.mjs
```

So our current workaround is to pin Nuxt to `4.4.6` until this is resolved.

### Expected behavior

The `node-server` production output should include all files referenced by package exports used by the generated renderer, especially `unhead/server -> dist/server.mjs`.

### Actual behavior

Nuxt `4.4.7` generates a server output that references `unhead/server`, packages `unhead@3.1.1`, but only includes a partial `dist` directory. This causes runtime SSR requests to fail with `ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND`.

issue.title: Support `0.x.y` and `0.0.x` versioning
issue.body:
Currently, uppt maps patch, minor and major bumps directly to x.y.z. But when the current version is 0.x.y, the situation is a bit different.

For example https://github.com/Teages/gazania/pull/28 have no breaking change but it got a major version bump. Maybe someone want the new feature get a major bump for 0.x.y but there's clear that we don't want a breaking change in early version to lead to the release of 1.0.0.

Following npm's behavior, maybe the bump behavior should be `0.major.[minor|patch]` and `0.0.[major|minor|patch]`. Should it be configurable?

- [x] I can make a PR for this if you want.

issue.title: vite-node dev server fails with `connect EINVAL` on macOS — socket path exceeds 104-byte sun_path limit (regression in 4.4.7)

labels.name: repo-724e8420
labels.description: Important issue, should be addressed soon

issue.body:
### Environment

- Nuxt: 4.4.7 (`@nuxt/vite-builder@4.4.7`)
- Works on: 4.4.6
- OS: macOS 26.5
- Node: 26.0.0
- Package manager: pnpm 10.25.0
- Default `TMPDIR` (`/var/folders/<…>/T`)

### Reproduction

Running `nuxt dev` on macOS with the default temp directory and opening the app fails on the first request:

```
ERROR  [request error] [unhandled] [GET] http://localhost:3000/
Error: connect EINVAL /var/folders/0m/<…>/T/nuxt-vite-node-PoNBaH/nuxt-vite-node-10112-<ts>.sock - Local (undefined:undefined)
    at internalConnect (node:net:1169:16)
    at attemptConnection (@nuxt/vite-builder/dist/vite-node.mjs:37:23)
      const socket = net.createConnection(viteNodeOptions.socketPath);
```

### Cause

In 4.4.7, `pickSocketPath` (`@nuxt/vite-builder/dist/index.mjs`) now wraps the socket file in a dedicated `mkdtemp` subdirectory (chmod `0700`) for isolation:

```js
// 4.4.7
const parentDir = fs.mkdtempSync(join(os.tmpdir(), "nuxt-vite-node-"));
fs.chmodSync(parentDir, 448);
return { socketPath: join(parentDir, `${socketName}.sock`), parentDir };
```

In 4.4.6 the socket file was placed directly in `os.tmpdir()`:

```js
// 4.4.6
return join(os.tmpdir(), `${socketName}.sock`);
```

The new intermediate `nuxt-vite-node-XXXXXX/` segment adds ~22 characters. On macOS the default `os.tmpdir()` is already long (`/var/folders/<…>/T`), so the full socket path now overruns the `sockaddr_un.sun_path` limit of **104 bytes**, and `net.createConnection()` fails with `EINVAL`.

Measured with the default macOS temp dir:

- 4.4.6 path length: **88** ✅ (under 104)
- 4.4.7 path length: **110** ❌ (over 104)

This is platform-dependent: shells/CI with a short `TMPDIR` stay under the limit and don't hit it, which is why it doesn't reproduce everywhere.

### Suggested fix

- Use a shorter `mkdtemp` prefix (e.g. `nvn-` instead of `nuxt-vite-node-`), and/or
- Detect when `os.tmpdir()` would make the resulting socket path exceed the platform limit and fall back (skip the subdir, or use a shorter base dir).

### Workaround

Run the dev server with a short `TMPDIR`:

```json
{ "scripts": { "dev": "TMPDIR=/tmp nuxt dev" } }
```

issue.title: Nuxt 4.4.7 node-server output packages incomplete unhead@3.1.1

labels.name: repo-724e8420
labels.description: Important issue, should be addressed soon

issue.body:
### Environment

- Nuxt version: `4.4.7`
- Nitro preset: `node-server`
- Package manager: `pnpm@10.33.1`
- Runtime: Node `v22.22.3`
- Deployment: built on server, run with `node .output/server/index.mjs` via PM2

### Describe the bug

After upgrading to Nuxt `4.4.7`, the production server build completes successfully, but the deployed app returns a runtime 500 on SSR requests.

Runtime error:

```text
Error [ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND]: Cannot find module '/home/stir-project/htdocs/releases/20260602185622/.output/server/node_modules/unhead/dist/server.mjs' imported from /home/stir-project/htdocs/releases/20260602185622/.output/server/chunks/routes/renderer.mjs
```

The generated renderer imports `unhead/server`:

```js
import { createHead, propsToString, renderSSRHead } from 'unhead/server'
```

But the packaged server output contains an incomplete `unhead@3.1.1` package:

```text
.output/server/node_modules/unhead -> .nitro/unhead@3.1.1
```

And inside the packaged `dist` directory only these files are present:

```text
.output/server/node_modules/unhead/dist/scripts.mjs
.output/server/node_modules/unhead/dist/shared/
```

Missing files include:

```text
.output/server/node_modules/unhead/dist/server.mjs
.output/server/node_modules/unhead/dist/index.mjs
.output/server/node_modules/unhead/dist/plugins.mjs
.output/server/node_modules/unhead/dist/utils.mjs
```

However, `.output/server/node_modules/unhead/package.json` still exposes:

```json
{
  "./server": {
    "types": "./dist/server.d.ts",
    "default": "./dist/server.mjs"
  }
}
```

So Node resolves `unhead/server` to a file that does not exist in the generated server output.

### Reproduction details

We have not yet reduced this to a minimal public reproduction, but this is consistently reproducible in our production deploy after the Nuxt `4.4.7` update.

The build itself reports success:

```text

✨ Build complete!
```

```text

output server.mjs OK
```

```text

.output/server/node_modules/unhead/dist/index.mjs
.output/server/node_modules/unhead/dist/plugins.mjs
.output/server/node_modules/unhead/dist/utils.mjs
```

So our current workaround is to pin Nuxt to `4.4.6` until this is resolved.

The `node-server` production output should include all files referenced by package exports used by the generated renderer, especially `unhead/server -> dist/server.mjs`.

### Actual behavior

Nuxt `4.4.7` generates a server output that references `unhead/server`, packages `unhead@3.1.1`, but only includes a partial `dist` directory. This causes runtime SSR requests to fail with `ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND`.

issue.title: fix: set helper on window in testing
issue.body:
### 🔗 Linked issue

resolves https://github.com/nuxt-modules/color-mode/issues/342
resolves #335

### ❓ Type of change

- [x] 🐞 Bug fix (a non-breaking change that fixes an issue)

### 📚 Description

previously we set helper but didn't make the helper available on the window object.

regression from https://github.com/nuxt-modules/color-mode/pull/325
